subject: Mathematics
title: Data Compression
description: A quick win: understand Data Compression.

hook.text: A song takes 50 megabytes uncompressed. An MP3 shrinks it to 5. Where did the other 45 go?

buildup.text: Compression removes redundant data. Instead of storing 'AAAA,' store 'A4.'

discovery.text: Lossless compression like ZIP preserves everything. Lossy formats like JPEG and MP3 discard what humans barely notice.

twist.text: JPEG removes high-frequency color detail your eyes can't resolve. The file shrinks but looks the same.

climax.text: Without compression, streaming video would need 100 times more bandwidth.

punchline.text: Math finds what's redundant so your files can shrink.

quiz.question: What is the key difference between lossless and lossy compression?
options: Lossless preserves all data; lossy discards some
